# host_file (Resource)

Manages whole host files or named Terraform-owned blocks inside host files.

## Example Usage

```terraform
resource "host_file" "zshrc" {
  path = "~/.zshrc"

  content = <<-EOT
    export EDITOR=nvim
    eval "$(starship init zsh)"
  EOT
}

resource "host_file" "zshrc_blocks" {
  path = "~/.zshrc"

  block {
    name    = "options"
    content = <<-EOT
      setopt autocd
      bindkey -v
    EOT
  }

  block {
    name = "alias"
  }

  block {
    name = "functions"
  }
}

resource "host_file_block" "foo_alias" {
  block   = host_file.zshrc_blocks.blocks.alias
  content = "alias foo=foobar"
}

resource "host_file_block" "bar_alias" {
  block   = host_file.zshrc_blocks.blocks.alias
  content = "alias bar=barbaz"
}

resource "host_file_block" "foo_function" {
  block   = host_file.zshrc_blocks.blocks.functions
  content = <<-EOT
    foo() { echo foo }
  EOT
}

resource "host_file_block" "bar_function" {
  block   = host_file.zshrc_blocks.blocks.functions
  content = <<-EOT
    bar() { echo bar }
  EOT
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `path` (String) Path to the host file. `~` is expanded to the current user's home directory when the provider reads or writes the file.

### Optional

- `block` (Block List) Named file block. Declaration order controls render order unless `before` or `after` is set. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--block))
- `content` (String) Full file content to manage without Terraform block markers. Mutually exclusive with `block`.

### Read-Only

- `blocks` (Attributes Map) Computed file block references for `host_file_block` resources. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--blocks))
- `id` (String) Resource identifier, equal to `path`.
- `rendered_content` (String) Last content observed in the host file. Used to detect drift.

<a id="nestedblock--block"></a>
### Nested Schema for `block`

Required:

- `name` (String) File block name.

Optional:

- `after` (List of String) Names of sibling file blocks that this block must be rendered after.
- `before` (List of String) Names of sibling file blocks that this block must be rendered before.
- `content` (String) Content managed directly by the host file block. `host_file_block` resources targeting the same block are rendered after this content.


<a id="nestedatt--blocks"></a>
### Nested Schema for `blocks`

Read-Only:

- `name` (String) File block name.
- `path` (String) Path to the host file that contains this block.
